explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PCV mode (PCVMode) or PSV mode (PSVMode), AND it is the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), AND an inspiratory pause is not active (!inspiratoryPause), AND the GUI has requested a Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton), THEN eventually (F) the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must be initiated or become active. This captures all the conditions specified in the natural language request: the operating modes, the specific timing (end of inspiration), the negative condition (no inspiratory pause required), the trigger from the GUI, and the eventual initiation of the RM procedure.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton -> F RM)

explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if two main conditions are met simultaneously: first, the system is operating in either Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode) or Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSVMode) (PCVMode | PSVMode); and second, it is the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) AND an inspiratory pause is not currently active or required (!inspiratoryPause), then it shall be possible to initiate a Lung Recruitment Maneuver (RM) if it is required by the GUI (RMButton). The combined condition `(PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton` forms the premise. The conclusion `F RM` indicates that under these conditions, the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) will eventually become active. The use of 'F' (eventually) captures the 'possible to initiate' aspect, implying that the procedure will commence at some point in the future once the preconditions are met and the GUI request is present. The choice of `!inspiratoryPause` instead of `!inspiratoryPauseButton` reflects that the *state* of not being in an inspiratory pause is required for RM initiation, rather than just the GUI not requesting it.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton -> F RM)

explanation: The LTL property ensures that a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is initiated under specific conditions. The condition "In PCV and PSV mode" is captured by "(PCVMode | PSVMode)", indicating that the system must be in either PCV or PSV mode. The phrase "at the end of an inspiration" is represented by "inspiratoryPhaseEnd". The condition "and if inspiratory pause is not required" is translated to "!inspiratoryPause". Finally, "if it is required by the GUI" is mapped to "RMButton", which signifies that the GUI has requested the RM procedure. The overall trigger for initiation is the conjunction of these four parts: "(PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton". The consequence "it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM)" implies an obligation for the system to start the RM. Given that "initiate" suggests an immediate action following the conditions, "X RM" is used, meaning RM becomes active in the very next state. Therefore, the global property "G(...)" ensures that whenever these conditions are met, the RM procedure is initiated in the subsequent state.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton -> X RM)

explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is operating in either PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode or PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, and the inspiratory phase has ended, and an inspiratory pause is not currently active, and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is requested by the GUI (RMButton), then it must eventually (F) be possible to initiate and activate the Recruitment Maneuver (RM). This captures the requirement that the RM procedure should be initiated under the specified conditions.
output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton) -> F RM)

explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the system is operating in either PCV Mode or PSV Mode, and the inspiratory phase has concluded (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and an inspiratory pause is not currently active (!inspiratoryPause), AND the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) has been requested via the GUI (RMButton), then it must eventually (F) be possible for the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) to become active.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton -> F RM)

explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in either PCV mode (PCVMode) or PSV mode (PSVMode), AND it is the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), AND an inspiratory pause is not set or required by the GUI (!inspiratoryPauseButton), AND a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is requested by the GUI (RMButton), THEN it shall eventually (F) be possible for the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) to become active. This captures all the specified conditions under which the RM procedure should be initiated upon GUI request.
output_LTL: G (( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton ) -> F RM)

explanation: The LTL property captures the requirement that a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) should be initiated under specific conditions. It states that globally (G), if the system is operating in either PCV Mode (PCVMode) or PSV Mode (PSVMode), and the current inspiratory phase has just ended (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and there is no request for an inspiratory pause from the GUI (!inspiratoryPauseButton), and the GUI has requested a Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton), then it shall eventually (F) be possible to initiate and activate the Recruitment Maneuver (RM). The 'PCV and PSV mode' is interpreted as 'PCV mode OR PSV mode' because the ventilator cannot be in both modes simultaneously. 'If inspiratory pause is not required' is mapped to '!inspiratoryPauseButton' as it refers to the GUI setting. 'If it is required by the GUI' for RM is mapped to 'RMButton'. 'It shall be possible to initiate' is captured by 'F RM', indicating that RM will eventually become active if the preceding conditions are met.
output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton) -> F RM)

explanation: The property states that globally, if the ventilator is operating in either PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode or PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, AND the inspiratory phase has ended, AND an inspiratory pause is not currently active or required, AND the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is requested by the GUI (Graphical User Interface), THEN eventually a lung recruitment procedure (RM) must be initiated. This captures the 'possible to initiate' aspect combined with the 'if required by the GUI' condition, meaning that if all these enabling conditions are met and the GUI requests it, the system will proceed to initiate RM.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton -> F RM)

explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode or PSV mode (`PCVMode | PSVMode`), and the inspiratory phase has ended (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`), and an inspiratory pause is not requested by the GUI (`!inspiratoryPauseButton`), and the GUI requests a Recruitment Maneuver (`RMButton`), then in the very next state (X), a Recruitment Maneuver (`RM`) must be initiated. This captures the requirement that the RM procedure shall be possible to initiate under the specified conditions when required by the GUI.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton -> X RM)

explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is operating in either PCV mode or PSV mode (PCVMode | PSVMode), and the inspiratory phase has just ended (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and an inspiratory pause is not currently active (!inspiratoryPause), AND the GUI has requested a Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton), then it must eventually be possible for the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) to become active (F RM). This accurately captures the condition-action relationship described in the natural language, ensuring that the RM procedure is initiated when all specified prerequisites are met.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause & RMButton -> F RM)
